easy.companyDescription: A mature enterprise software company shifting more of its business toward cloud subscriptions.
easy.macroContext: Businesses are moving more computing work from owned infrastructure to rented cloud services.
easy.situation: Can a large installed base revive growth through subscriptions before faster rivals define the market?
easy.longCase: Long-standing corporate relationships could accelerate adoption of cloud and subscription offerings.
easy.shortCase: Mature product lines may shrink before replacements contribute enough growth to satisfy investors.
setupHints: Years of investor frustration raise the bar for whether this pivot looks credible.

medium.companyDescription: A diversified technology vendor selling mainly to businesses.
medium.macroContext: Corporate buyers are changing how they purchase and consume software and infrastructure.
medium.situation: Can the firm refresh growth before mature offerings slow too far?
medium.longCase: Deep customer relationships could help introduce newer offerings at lower acquisition cost.
medium.shortCase: Legacy dependence and faster-moving rivals can keep growth muted longer than investors tolerate.

hard.macroContext: Business technology spending is being reallocated across old and new delivery models.
hard.situation: Will an established vendor convert its footprint into renewed growth in time?
hard.longCase: An existing commercial footprint can lower the cost of distributing newer products.
hard.shortCase: If replacements ramp slowly, mature-line pressure can dominate the equity story.

reveal.shortText: That was Microsoft. The cloud pivot under Satya Nadella started changing how investors valued the old software giant.
reveal.funFact: By early 2016, the market had started rewarding the company for cloud growth and recurring software revenue.
whyItMoved: Cloud and subscription revenue became more visible.
Investors began re-rating recurring software streams.
Enterprise relationships helped distribution of newer offerings.

sources.label: Prototype sample seed
sources.notes: Placeholder sample content for Phase 3 pipeline; not production research.

review.reviewNotes: Medium/Hard rewritten after H023 Gate 2 identity failures (multi-line vendor + commercial relationships → subscription triangulation). Intended peer set: Microsoft / Oracle / IBM / SAP / Cisco-class enterprise vendors. Easy unchanged; Medium/Hard Gate 2 pending re-judge.

guesses.pointingFact: Jan 2014 mature enterprise software giant shifting to cloud subscriptions after years of investor frustration
Large enterprise software vendor converting licenses to cloud/subscription
Mature enterprise tech firm trying to revive growth as IT moves to rented cloud services
Enterprise software installed-base story pivoting toward subscription offerings
Software company known for subscription transition from mature product lines

direction.cue: Broad corporate installed base can accelerate cloud subscription adoption if the pivot is credible
